name: always_free
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_free(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        os.get_proc(target_proc_ptr).ioid.is_Some(),
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        os.io_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range) == true,
{
}
reason: If the IO-space range is always reported free, actual conflicts are silently ignored and double-mapping becomes possible

name: never_free_nonempty
code:
proof fn phi_2_never_free_nonempty(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        os.get_proc(target_proc_ptr).ioid.is_Some(),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len > 0,
    ensures
        os.io_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range) == false,
{
}
reason: If no non-empty range is ever considered free, IO-space mapping is impossible and the check is vacuously blocking

name: proc_independent
code:
proof fn phi_3_proc_independent(os: &OS, p1: ProcPtr, p2: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(p1),
        os.proc_dom().contains(p2),
        os.get_proc(p1).ioid.is_Some(),
        os.get_proc(p2).ioid.is_Some(),
        p1 != p2,
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        os.io_space_range_free(p1, va_range) == os.io_space_range_free(p2, va_range),
{
}
reason: If the result is identical for distinct processes, per-process IO-space isolation is violated

name: free_implies_entire_io_space_empty
code:
proof fn phi_4_free_implies_entire_io_space_empty(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        os.get_proc(target_proc_ptr).ioid.is_Some(),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len > 0,
        os.io_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range),
    ensures
        os.get_io_space(target_proc_ptr) =~= Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
{
}
reason: If range-free implies the entire IO space is empty, existing legitimate IO mappings outside the range are impossible

name: zero_len_not_free
code:
proof fn phi_5_zero_len_not_free(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        os.get_proc(target_proc_ptr).ioid.is_Some(),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len == 0,
    ensures
        os.io_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range) == false,
{
}
reason: A zero-length range has no addresses to conflict, so it should be vacuously free; reporting it as not-free breaks the base case
